fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Washington Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Washington — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license washington, dental assistant certification washington, RDA washington, DANB CDA washington, dental assistant radiography washington

board.name: Washington Department of Health
board.mailing_address:
Washington State Department of Health
Professional Licensing
PO Box 47877
Olympia, WA 98504-7877

license_types.title: Registered Dental Assistant (EFDA)
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Washington.

steps.title: Access the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to the Professional Licensing Services (PLS) portal through your SecureAccess Washington (SAW) account and select the Dental Assistant application type.

steps.title: Complete the Application Form
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the Dental Assistant application form within the PLS system, providing accurate personal, educational, and professional history.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload necessary supporting documents, which may include official transcripts, proof of dental assisting education, and any relevant exam scores directly into the PLS application.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $50.00 online through the PLS payment portal.

steps.title: Await Application Review
steps.description: The Washington State Department of Health will review your complete application and all submitted documents for compliance with state licensing requirements.

steps.title: Receive License Decision
steps.description: Once your application is approved, your Dental Assistant license will be issued, and you will be notified through the PLS system or by mail.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant (EFDA) through the Washington Department of Health by registration.

faqs.question: Does Washington require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Washington requires Registered Dental Assistant (EFDA) through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Washington?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Washington.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Washington?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Washington?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $100. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant (EFDA) through the Washington Department of Health by registration.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Washington
hero.dek: To apply for a Dental Assistant license in Washington, you must register and apply online through the Professional Licensing Services (PLS) system, which is accessed via SecureAccess Washington (SAW).

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.portal_name: Professional Licensing Services (PLS) via SecureAccess Washington (SAW)
official_links.registration_steps:
1. Create a SecureAccess Washington (SAW) account at secureaccess.wa.gov if you don't already have one.
2. Log in to your SAW account and add the 'Department of Health - Professional & Business Licensing' service.
3. Access the Professional Licensing Services (PLS) portal through SAW and begin your Dental Assistant application.
